# rhino {#sec-rhino} :::: {.callout-warning collapse='true' appearance='default' icon=false} ## [TLDR   ![](images/rhino.png){width='10%'}]{style='font-weight: bold; font-size: 1.05em;'} ::: {style='font-size: 0.95em; color: #282b2d;'}
**WARNING**: `rhino` isn't like the previous two frameworks we've covered in this section, because `rhino` doesn't create an app-package:
- `rhino` apps rely on [`renv`](https://rstudio.github.io/renv/articles/renv.html) and [`box`](https://klmr.me/box/) for managing imported dependencies (instead of the `DESCRIPTION` and `NAMESPACE` files in an R package). - `rhino` requires [node.js](https://www.wikiwand.com/en/Node.js), an open-source JavaScript runtime environment. ::: :::: This chapter briefly describes a version of `sap` built using the [`rhino`](https://appsilon.github.io/rhino/) framework. The branch in this chapter is slightly different than the previous `golem` and `leprechaun` frameworks, because `rap` is not an R package. If you're developing in RStudio ![](images/rstudio-icon.png){height=30}, instead of loading, documenting, and installing `rap`, we're going to re-initialize the IDE by selecting **Session** > **Terminate R...** ![Re-initialize the IDE](images/rhino_session_terminate.png){width='30%' fig-align='center'} When RStudio ![](images/rstudio-icon.png){height=30} re-opens, we see the `rap` files and notice the **Build** pane has been removed: ![`rhino` app IDE](images/rhino_app_ide.png){width='100%' fig-align='center'} To reiterate, the **Build** pane is deactivated because **`rhino` applications aren't R packages**.[^rhino-terminate] Launch the application in `rap` by opening the `app.R` file and clicking **Run App** (or by passing `rhino::app()` into the **Console**). :::: {.column-margin} Launch app with the [`shinypak` package](https://mjfrigaard.github.io/shinypak/articles/getting-started.html): :::{style='font-size: 0.65rem;'} ```r launch('25_rhino') ``` ::: :::: ![Calling `rhino::app()`](images/rhino_run_app.png){width='100%' fig-align='center'} ::::{.callout-tip collapse='true' appearance='deafult'} ## [Access the applications in this chapter]{style='font-weight: bold; font-size: 1.15em;'} :::{style='font-size: 0.95em; color: #282b2d;'} I've created the [`shinypak` R package](https://mjfrigaard.github.io/shinypak/) in an effort to make each section accessible and easy to follow. Install `shinypak` using `pak` (or `remotes`): ```r install.packages('pak') pak::pak('mjfrigaard/shinypak') library(shinypak) ``` List the apps in this chapter: ```r list_apps(regex = '^25') ``` Launch apps with `launch()` ```r launch(app = '25_rhino') ``` Download apps with `get_app()` ```r get_app(app = '25_rhino') ``` :::: ::: [^rhino-terminate]: I re-initialize the session on the `25_rhino` branch so I'm not tempted to load, document, install, or test the code using the IDE. ## `rap` (a `rhino` app) The files in `rap` are below: ```{.default} ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ .git ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ .github # <1> ‚îÇ ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ workflows ‚îÇ ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ rhino-test.yml # <1> ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ .gitignore ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ .lintr # <2> ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ .renvignore # <3> ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ .rhino # <4> ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ .eslintrc.json ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ .gitignore ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ .stylelintrc.json ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ babel.config.json ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ node_modules ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ package-lock.json ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ package.json ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ prettier.config.mjs ‚îÇ ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ webpack.config.js # <4> ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ .Rhistory ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ .Rprofile # <5> ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ .rscignore ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ app ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ js ‚îÇ ‚îÇ ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ index.js ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ logic ‚îÇ ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ __init__.R ‚îÇ ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ data.R ‚îÇ ‚îÇ ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ plot.R ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ main.R ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ static ‚îÇ ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ css ‚îÇ ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ favicon.ico ‚îÇ ‚îÇ ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ js ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ styles ‚îÇ ‚îÇ ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ main.scss ‚îÇ ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ view ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ __init__.R ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ aes_inputs.R ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ clicks.R ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ display.R ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ message.R ‚îÇ ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ var_inputs.R ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ app.R ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ config.yml ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ dependencies.R ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ README.md ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ renv # <6> ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ .gitignore ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ activate.R ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ library ‚îÇ ‚îÇ ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ macos ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ settings.json ‚îÇ ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ staging # <6> ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ renv.lock ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ rhino.yml ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ sap.Rproj ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ tests ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ cypress ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ .gitignore ‚îÇ ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ e2e ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ cypress.config.js ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ testthat ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ _snaps ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ setup-shinytest2.R ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ test-aes_inputs.R ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ test-data.R ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ test-display.R ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ test-main.R ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ test-plot.R ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ test-rap-feature-01.R ‚îÇ ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ test-shinytest2.R ‚îÇ ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ test-var_inputs.R ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ testthat.R ``` 1. CI/CD via [GitHub actions](https://github.com/r-lib/actions) 2. Lintr (from [`lintr`](https://lintr.r-lib.org/) package) 3. `renv` ignore (works like `.gitignore`) 4. `rhino` app dependencies 5. Activates the [`renv` package](https://rstudio.github.io/renv/articles/renv.html) 6. `renv` library of packages in app project As we can see, most of the standard R package folders and files are missing from `rap`, because `rhino` applications use the [`box` package](https://klmr.me/box/) for importing dependencies and organizing code.[^rhino-box-depends] ## `rhino` features {#sec-rhino-features} The [`rhino` website](https://appsilon.github.io/rhino/articles/explanation/application-structure.html) explains the philosophy behind the application structure above, so I won't repeat that information here. However, I highly recommend reading the available documentation on [`rhino`](https://appsilon.github.io/rhino/articles/tutorial/create-your-first-rhino-app.html) and [`box`](https://klmr.me/box/articles/box.html) before deciding to adopt this framework.[^rhino-recommended-documentation] [^rhino-box-depends]: Imported dependencies in `rhino` apps use [`box` modules](https://klmr.me/box/articles/box.html) instead of the `DESCRIPTION` and `NAMESPACE`. [^rhino-recommended-documentation]: Be sure to read up on [testing box modules](https://klmr.me/box/articles/testing.html) and `rhino` applications [with cypress](https://appsilon.github.io/rhino/articles/tutorial/write-end-to-end-tests-with-cypress.html) and [`shinytest2`](https://appsilon.github.io/rhino/articles/how-to/use-shinytest2.html). ## `box` modules {#sec-rhino-box} A `box` module (not to be confused with a Shiny module) is a collection of `.R` scripts in a specified folder. The modules in a new `rhino` app are stored in the `app/logic/` and `app/view/` folders:[^rhino-code-structure] [^rhino-code-structure]: `rhino` [recommends](https://appsilon.github.io/rhino/articles/explanation/application-structure.html#philosophy) placing non-Shiny code in the `app/logic` folder and keeping all Shiny modules and reactive code in `app/view`. ::: {.cell} ```{.bash .cell-code code-fold="false"} app ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ js/ # <1> ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ logic/ # <2> ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ main.R # <3> ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ static/ # <4> ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ styles/ # <5> ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ view/ # <6> 6 directories, 1 file ``` ::: 1. JavaScript code 2. Non-shiny code 3. Primary app file 4. Static `.js` or `.css` 5. App CSS files 6. Shiny modules and app code ### Utility functions {#sec-rhino-utils} In `rap`, I've placed the non-Shiny utility functions (i.e., the business logic) in `app/logic`: ::: {.cell} ```{.bash .cell-code code-fold="false"} app/logic ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ __init__.R ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ data.R # <1> ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ plot.R # <2> 1 directory, 4 files ``` ::: 1. Load `movies` data 2. `scatter_plot()` utility function ### Shiny modules {#sec-rhino-modules} Our Shiny `box` modules are placed in `app/view`, and separated into `inputs` and `display`: ::: {.cell} ```{.bash .cell-code code-fold="false"} app/view ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ __init__.R ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ aes_inputs.R # <1> ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ display.R # <2> ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ var_inputs.R # <3> 1 directory, 6 files ``` ::: 1. similar to the code from `R/mod_aes_input.R` 2. similar to the code from `R/mod_scatter_display.R` 3. similar to the code from `R/mod_var_input.R` `app/view/aes_inputs` and `app/view/var_inputs` collect and return the reactive values from the UI. The `app/view/display` module includes the `app/logic/data` and `app/logic/plot` modules. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="false"} # app/view/display.R # import data and plot modules box::use( app / logic / data, app / logic / plot ) ``` ::: ## [`app/main.R`]{style="font-size: 1.05em;"} {#sec-rhino-app-r} The `app/main.R` file contains the primary UI and Server functions for the application. This file adds the `shiny` functions *and* the `aes_inputs`, `var_inputs`, and `display` modules from `app/view`: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="false"} # app/main.R # shiny functions box::use( shiny[ NS, span, tags, icon, moduleServer ], # bslib functions bslib[ page_fillable, layout_sidebar, sidebar, card, card_header, card_body, bs_theme ] ) # import modules ---- box::use( ## load inputs module app / view / var_inputs, app / view / aes_inputs, ## load display module app / view / display ) ``` ::: Note that we don't need to import `app/logic` modules in `app/main.R`, because they're imported in their respective `app/view` modules. ## Tests {#sec-rhino-tests} `rhino` apps have support for testing with `testthat`, `shiny::testServer()`, `shinytest2`, and [Cypress](https://appsilon.github.io/rhino/articles/tutorial/write-end-to-end-tests-with-cypress.html). ::: {.cell} ```{.bash .cell-code code-fold="false"} ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ tests ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ cypress # <1> ‚îÇ ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ e2e ‚îÇ ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ app.cy.js ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ cypress.config.js # <1> ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ testthat # <2> ‚îÇ ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ test-main.R ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ testthat.R # <2> ``` ::: 1. Cypress test infrastructure 1. `testthat` test infrastructure Below is the boilerplate test code in the `tests/testthat/test-main.R` file: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="false"} box::use( # <1> shiny[testServer], testthat[...], ) box::use( app/main[...], ) # <1> test_that("main server works", { # <2> testServer(server, { expect_equal(output$message, "Hello!") }) }) # <2> ``` ::: 1. `box` module importing test package functions 2. Using `shiny::testServer()` and `testthat::test_that()` functions in test. I've included tests for the utility functions and modules in the `25_rhino` branch, but I'll cover testing with rhino elsewhere.[^testing-rhino-apps] [^testing-rhino-apps]: See the [Shiny frameworks](https://mjfrigaard.github.io/sfw/) supplemental website for more information on testing your `rhino` app. ## `rhino` dependencies {#sec-rhino-depends} In `rhino` apps, dependencies are managed by [`renv`](https://rstudio.github.io/renv/articles/renv.html) and the `dependencies.R` file. The `renv` package is designed to, > *"create[s] and manage[s] project-local R libraries, save[s] the state of these libraries to a 'lockfile', and later restore[s] the library as required."* [^rhino-renv-description] The `rhino::pkg_install()` helper function updates *both* the `dependencies.R` file and `renv` library. Using `dependencies.R`, `renv`, and `box` modules removes the need to manage dependencies in a `DESCRIPTION` or `NAMESPACE` file.[^rhino-renv-config] [^rhino-renv-description]: As described in `renv`'s [DESCRIPTION file](https://github.com/rstudio/renv/blob/main/DESCRIPTION) [^rhino-renv-config]: Be sure to read the [`renv` configuration article](https://appsilon.github.io/rhino/articles/explanation/renv-configuration.html) for a better understanding on how it works with rhino apps. ## Recap {.unnumbered} :::: {.callout-tip collapse='false' appearance='default' icon=false} ## [RECAP   ![](images/rhino.png){width='10%'}]{style='font-weight: bold; font-size: 1.05em;'} ::: {style='font-size: 0.95em; color: #282b2d;'}
`rhino` takes a novel and innovative approach to developing Shiny applications (and covering all the ways they differ from app-packages is beyond the scope of this book). Feel free to review the code in the [`25_rhino` branch](https://github.com/mjfrigaard/sap/tree/25_rhino) for a better understanding of how the `box` modules are structured and used within the `ui` and `server`. The `rhino` framework isn't used as wildly `golem`,[^rhino-cran-downloads] but it's been gaining popularity (and has been used in a recent [pilot FDA submission](https://github.com/appsilon/rhino-fda-pilot)). ![`rhino` CRAN downloads](images/rhino_cran_downloads.png){width='100%' fig-align='center'} ::: :::: :::: {.callout-note collapse='true' appearance='default' icon=false} ## [See a typo, error, or something missing?]{style='font-weight: bold; font-size: 1.05em;'} ::: {style='font-size: 0.95em; color: #282b2d;'} Please open an issue on [GitHub](https://github.com/mjfrigaard/shiny-app-pkgs/issues/new) ::: :::: [^rhino-cran-downloads]: Check for yourself on [cran-downloads](https://hadley.shinyapps.io/cran-downloads/)
